compliance_rules: NO funds flow from FP to EAF — IRS private benefit prohibition
MONK IP belongs to MONK LLC only — never EAF or FP property
NDAA fleet segregation — Green UAS never mixed with non-NDAA hardware on government contracts
Red team exercises require written authorization + James Scott Esq. review before execution
EAF compliance always beats FP efficiency when they conflict
DJI M350 RTK for private sector only — never on government contract
Fleet 3 show hardware — private entertainment only, never government contracts
Fleet 4 FPV — private EAF property and contracted private-sector red team only

V6.name: Fleet Services, DaaS & Inside the Fence

V7.name: Technology Commercialization — MONK, AKMS, Data Products

MONK.description: Hardware-anchored trust layer for autonomous drone operations. Cryptographically signed mission authorization, authenticated identity broadcast, tamper-resistant mission evidence.
MONK.status: Prototype v1.0 in development. Provisional patent pending. MONK LLC formation in progress.
MONK.sbir_target: DHS S&T or DoD — ~$175,000 Phase I. Pending Congressional reauthorization.
MONK.bvlos_relevance: MONK safety architecture is the core of Part 108 compliance case. TSA security requirements in BVLOS NPRM directly favor MONK-equipped fleets.

AKMS.description: Software OS for High-Definition Autonomous Swarms and BVLOS logistics. Data moat compounds with every flight.
AKMS.data_value: 250 drones x show hours x red team exercises = proprietary flight dataset no competitor can replicate

CAKO.description: 12-16 week EAF-run zero-tuition certification: FAA Part 107, Security+, CAPM, PCEP. Trains underserved Baltimore community members as professional drone operators.
CAKO.pipeline: Each cohort adds certified operators to FP Talent Pipeline Agreement
